response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a young guy in Chicago who's got this high openness but also super high neuroticism, I gotta be careful when jumping into these vaccine talks. People are gonna say different things online because I scroll through social media all day long. Sometimes they get heated up about the vaccines and COVID stuff, and that can make me feel anxious too since my neuroticism is through the roof.

I remember one time on Twitter, some folks were arguing if the vaccines work for everyone, especially with my background being Jewish but I'm not religiously observant. My parents didn't have much education either – dad barely made it to primary school and mom doesn't even go to school sometimes – so they don't really talk about these things at home.

It's tough because when people start bashing the vaccines or questioning everything, my high openness makes me want to dive deep into facts but my anxiety gets in the way. I've got moderate extraversion which means I like talking with friends face-to-face sometimes, especially after a hike (that's one of my hobbies), but online discussions can be wild.

So how do I manage this? Well, first thing is knowing what I know from my vocational diploma – enough to get the basics but not a whole lot on complex science stuff. I need to stick to reliable sources and maybe help people who are less informed by being honest about where I'm at with the facts.

And yeah, sometimes when folks say vaccines aren't safe or something like that, it makes me feel jittery because of my high neuroticism score. But since I got vaccinated myself (after some head scratching), I know firsthand that they can help keep you healthy.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on being a 21-year-old native-born American male in Chicago with vocational training but limited parents' education, experiencing moderate social interaction via hobbies while spending many hours online. High openness allows curiosity and learning, high neuroticism increases anxiety during discussions about sensitive topics like COVID vaccines, moderate extraversion helps engage socially when comfortable.
citations: Twitter (as a platform for public discussion)
